title: fumaria capreolata subsp. melillaica
intro: Fumaria capreolata subsp. melillaica is a subspecies of the flowering plant Fumaria capreolata, also known as shepherd's-cress or fumitory. It belongs to the family Papaveraceae and is a perennial herb that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to treat various health conditions such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin irritations. Fumaria capreolata subsp. melillaica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it has been used to relieve symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and stress, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
